construct_verdict_reason: Most items measure attitudes toward economic globalization and trade, but p49_1 shifts focus to a specific sectoral foreign investment policy question that may reflect regulatory/nationalist concerns distinct from general trade attitudes.

items.reason: Directly measures evaluation of free trade agreements' impact on Mexican economy, core to economic globalization attitudes.
Directly measures perspective on whether foreign trade benefits the Mexican economy, central to economic globalization attitudes.
Directly measures agreement with free trade agreements, a key component of economic globalization attitudes.

Measures approval of foreign investment in a specific strategic sector (telecommunications) rather than general attitudes toward foreign investment or economic globalization, conflating sectoral protectionism with broader trade orientation.
Directly measures evaluation of bilateral trade relationship with the US, explicitly mentioned in construct description as part of economic globalization attitudes.
